Schedule

Five days of practical automation lessons.

Explore each day’s topic, learning goals, and assignments. Use the search field to find lessons quickly.

Showing 5 of 5 days

Day 1

Python Basics for Automation

Variables, loops, functions, file handling, and a file organizer project.

Overview

Begin with the essential building blocks for automation scripts and learn how Python manages data and files.

Learning objectives

  • Understand variables and data types
  • Write loops and functions
  • Read and write files
  • Build a file organizer

Code example

import os

source_dir = 'workspace/files'
for file_name in os.listdir(source_dir):
    if file_name.endswith('.pdf'):
        target_folder = os.path.join(source_dir, 'PDFs')
        os.makedirs(target_folder, exist_ok=True)
        os.rename(os.path.join(source_dir, file_name), os.path.join(target_folder, file_name))

Resources

Python file I/O docsPathlib referenceMini project starter template

Assignment

Create a folder sorter

Beginner

Write a script that moves files into type-based folders in a target directory.

Est. 30 minSubmit via GitHub link

Day 2

CSV & Excel Automation

Automate spreadsheets using pandas and openpyxl for attendance and marks.

Overview

Learn how to read, update, and save tabular data so automation works with real-world spreadsheets.

Learning objectives

  • Load CSV files
  • Edit Excel sheets
  • Use pandas for data cleanup
  • Build attendance automation

Code example

import pandas as pd

attendance = pd.read_csv('attendance.csv')
attendance['Status'] = attendance['Hours'].apply(lambda hours: 'Present' if hours >= 3 else 'Absent')
attendance.to_excel('attendance_report.xlsx', index=False)

Resources

pandas cheat sheetopenpyxl quickstartCSV best practices

Assignment

Attendance sheet updater

Beginner

Use pandas to add a status column and save the result to a new CSV file.

Est. 40 minSubmit via GitHub link

Day 3

Web Scraping & APIs

Collect data from web pages and API responses with requests and JSON parsing.

Overview

Build scripts that fetch web content, parse HTML, and consume API endpoints for automation workflows.

Learning objectives

  • Fetch web pages
  • Parse HTML content
  • Read JSON APIs
  • Build weather/news automation

Code example

import requests

response = requests.get('https://api.example.com/weather')
if response.ok:
    data = response.json()
    print(f"Temperature: {data['temp']}°C")

Resources

requests guideBeautifulSoup tipsJSON parsing examples

Assignment

API weather summary

Beginner

Write a script that turns JSON weather data into a simple summary message.

Est. 35 minSubmit via GitHub link

Day 4

Email & Notification Automation

Automate emails, schedule tasks, and prepare a bot notification workflow.

Overview

Explore how scripts can send alerts and work with scheduling for repeatable automations.

Learning objectives

  • Send emails with smtplib
  • Build scheduled jobs
  • Create simple Telegram bot logic
  • Trigger notifications

Code example

from datetime import datetime

reminder = 'Submit the project report'
print(f"[{datetime.now()}] Reminder: {reminder}")

Resources

smtplib examplecron and schedule tipsTelegram bot starter guide

Assignment

Build a reminder sender

Beginner

Write a script that formats a reminder message and logs the notification payload.

Est. 45 minSubmit via GitHub link

Day 5

Mini Projects & Deployment

Bring everything together with a final tool and learn GitHub basics for sharing code.

Overview

Build a polished automation tool, prepare a GitHub repo, and explore simple deployment steps.

Learning objectives

  • Design a final automation workflow
  • Prepare GitHub projects
  • Share code professionally
  • Deploy Python scripts

Code example

def send_report(summary):
    print('Final automation report:')
    print(summary)

send_report('File organizer and attendance tracker ready.')

Resources

GitHub repo checklistdeployment notesproject submission guide

Assignment

Final automation tool plan

Beginner

Write a project summary and identify the files, libraries, and steps needed to build your tool.

Est. 50 minSubmit via GitHub link
